channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces said they shot down a Russian fighter plane on Saturday while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Militants kill 6 Pakistani soldiers in a shootout
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Militants killed six Pakistani soldiers in a shootout, the army said Saturday, the latest unrest in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan where armed groups including the Pakistani Taliban are active.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European cities brace for pro-Palestinian rallies amid high alert as Oct. 7 anniversary approaches
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of pro-Palestinian demonstrators are expected to take to the streets in rallies across Europe demanding a cease-fire, as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approaches.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano And Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US downsizing in Iraq sparks fear from Kurdish minority
item.description: Kurdish minorities are sounding the alarm over an expected U.S. downsizing from Iraq by 2026, worried they will be left vulnerable to extremist attacks that have frequently targeted the group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Rescue teams search for missing after floods, landslides kill at least 16 in Bosnia
item.description: JABLANICA, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — Rescue teams on Saturday searched for those still missing after flash floods and landslides hit parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people and injuring scores more.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers urge Biden to seek release of Americans detained in China
item.description: Two congressmen asked President Biden to further advocate for the release of wrongfully detained Americans in China in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran's supreme leader makes rare speech after attacks on Israel
item.description: Iran's leader defended his country’s missile strikes on Israel this week, calling them “correct, logical, and lawful” in a rare public speech in Tehran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military kills 250 Hezbollah terrorists since start of limited ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military says it has killed 250 Hezbollah terrorists and has struck more than 2,000 military targets since it launched a limited ground operation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh in on Mexico's lawsuit against US gun manufacturers
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take up the gun industry’s appeal of a ruling allowing the Mexican government to proceed with its lawsuit against several prominent American firearm manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Susan Rice: 'Surrender monkey' Trump is GOP's Neville Chamberlain
item.description: Former White House adviser Susan Rice criticized former President Trump over his foreign policy agenda, calling him an "appeaser" and likening him to former British Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain — who tried to appease Nazi leader Adolf Hitler.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Clashes in Kenya as people discuss the deputy president's impeachment motion
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Supporters and opponents of Kenya's deputy president clashed Friday at public forums over an impeachment motion against him, which was introduced in parliament by the ruling alliance this week.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei defends missile barrage against Israel in rare sermon
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei said during a rare public speech Friday that his country's missile attack on Israel this week was "legal and legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Should Israel attack Iran's nuclear facilities? Biden, GOP disagree
item.description: The debate over whether Israel should attack Iran's nuclear facilities is roiling Washington as the Biden administration seeks to temper its ally’s response to Tehran’s missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Rainstorms and heavy floods hit large parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people
item.description: Severe rainstorms have struck Bosnia overnight, killing at least 16 people and flooding several towns and villages in central and southern parts of the country.
item.dc:creator: Eldar Emric, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: The latest comments come during heightened animosities over the North’s recent disclosure of a nuclear facility.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a "listening device" in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017. Johnson had been a foreign secretary at the time.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 teens detained following explosions near Denmark's Israeli Embassy
item.description: Two Swedish teenagers were detained Thursday on preliminary charges in connection with a pair of explosions near the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus opposition urges immediate release of over 200 political prisoners in dire state
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Belarusian opposition leader in exile Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya on Thursday said 224 political prisoners in Belarus must be freed urgently, and called on Western countries to negotiate their release with the Belarusian authorities.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran warns of 'decisive response' if Israel crosses 'red lines'
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned this week at the Asia Cooperation Dialogue summit that Israel cannot act with "impunity" and "crossing [Iran's] red lines" will be met with a "decisive response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese Communist Party marks 75 years of rule as economic, security threats persist
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule. The state-controlled media reported on China's progress and stability, with no mention of the many challenges facing the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will Netanyahu 'take the fight directly to Iran' after massive missile strike across Israel?
item.description: Amid the most lethal Iranian missile strike into Israel territory, military experts are urging Israel to take off the gloves and punish the mullahs in Iran with devastating warfare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli troops battle Hezbollah in Lebanon as fears of a wider war mount after Iran's missile attack
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli troops battled Hezbollah militants inside Lebanon on Wednesday after launching what they say are limited ground operations there.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9
